question.text: In June 2015, what extension was made to the IRF product range?

answer.text: Cash-settled futures were extended to 6-year and 13-year GOI securities
Options on 10-year GOI bond futures were introduced
Physical delivery was reintroduced for all IRF contracts
MIBOR futures were launched for the first time

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** In June 2015, following the success of the 10-year GOI bond futures launched in December 2013, the framework was extended to include 6-year and 13-year GOI securities. This provided hedging instruments across more maturity points on the yield curve.
**The Trap:** Options on IRF exist but their specific launch details differ. Physical delivery was not reintroduced. MIBOR futures had a different launch timeline. The 2015 extension specifically added the 6-year and 13-year tenors.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The expansion to multiple tenors (6Y, 10Y, 13Y) allows for yield curve trading strategies (steepeners, flatteners) using futures. This is a significant development for Indian fixed income markets.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The three tenors (6Y, 10Y, 13Y) roughly correspond to the short, medium, and long end of the GOI yield curve. This allows targeted hedging — a bank with a 5-7 year bond portfolio would use 6-year futures, while an insurer with 12-15 year liabilities would use 13-year futures.
